changes.motivation: Environment flake.nix files (development, management, test, production) and the CLI template were using ~220-line inline reimplementations of the devshell instead of the `mkDevShell` abstraction already exposed by the root flake's `lib`.
changes.summary: Replaced all environment flake.nix files and the CLI template flake.nix with a minimal ~16-line pattern using `panfactum.lib.${system}.mkDevShell { name = "..."; }`, mirroring how the installer generates flake.nix files.

changes.motivation: Initial replacement used `root` as the flake input name but the correct convention matching the installer is `panfactum`.
changes.summary: Renamed the flake input from `root` to `panfactum` in the CLI template and all environment flake.nix files, with `panfactum.url = "path:../.."` for within-repo references.

changes.motivation: The root flake.nix was inadvertently modified during the simplification attempt, breaking its more nuanced `mkDevShell` usage that merges in `localDevShell` packages and hooks.
changes.summary: Restored root flake.nix to the last committed state via `git checkout HEAD -- flake.nix`.

changes.motivation: Users customizing environment devshells needed guidance on how to add extra packages or shell hooks beyond the Panfactum defaults.
changes.summary: Added commented-out `packages` and `shellHook` parameters to the CLI flake.nix template, showing the extension points that `mkDevShell` already supports.

problems: The CLI template flake.nix and all environment flake.nix files were completely out of sync with the installer's generated flake.nix — one used ~220 lines of inline nixpkgs wiring while the other used a 5-line `mkDevShell` call. There was no documentation or comment explaining that `mkDevShell` is the intended public API for environment flakes, making this divergence hard to notice until runtime.

When renaming the flake input from `root` to `panfactum`, the Edit tool failed on the management/flake.nix due to the change already being partially applied, requiring a full Write to recover. Multi-step rename operations on files that have just been written are fragile; using replace_all or doing a single Write is more reliable.

The `mkDevShell` function accepts a `packages` parameter, but passing nixpkgs packages requires knowing the correct flake attribute path (`panfactum.legacyPackages.${system}` vs something else). This is undocumented in the template and in the root flake — a user would have to read the root flake source to figure out how to reference nixpkgs packages.
